text.title: QA Suite Health Metrics Canon Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-suite-health-metrics-canon methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Health is reported as exactly 6 metrics. Adding a 7th requires a methodology version bump and a deprecation of one existing.
rule.rationale: Open metric sets become noise; closed sets remain comparable across releases.

rule.statement: Each metric carries an absolute threshold AND a desired direction (lower-is-better or higher-is-better).
rule.rationale: Without threshold there's no breach; without direction trend tracking is ambiguous.

rule.statement: Each metric names one owner (handle, not team) accountable for breach response.
Metrics are computed from CI artefacts, not manual entries.
rule.rationale: Manual KPIs are aspirational; CI-computed ones are auditable.

rule.statement: Report is regenerated weekly; older reports stay immutable for trend analysis.
rule.rationale: Lower cadence misses regressions; higher cadence creates noise without signal.

rule.statement: Each metric breach is routed to a named remediation methodology (flake → flake-ledger; runtime → perf-test-tools; coverage → test-strategy).
rule.rationale: KPI without remediation path is decorative.

rule.statement: If suite <100 tests OR no CI metrics platform OR single-team informal works, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Canon overhead is wasted at small scale.
